name: Sierra del Pobo and Camino de los Pilones – circular from Aguilar de Alfambra
name_original: Sierra del Pobo y Camino de los Pilones – circular desde Aguilar de Alfambra
description: This difficult 34.5-mile touring cycling route from Aguilar de Alfambra explores the Sierra del Pobo and historic Camino de los Pilones.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Aguilar Del Alfambra offers routes through a diverse landscape in the province of Teruel, Spain. The area is characterized by the Alfambra River, which has carved geological formations like the Cluse del Alfambra, and features unique "chopos cabeceros" (pollard willows) along its banks. Cyclists can expect varied terrains, including the rugged Sierra del Pobo, riverine ecosystems, and agricultural fields. This region is recognized for its geological significance and natural beauty. Best touring cycling routes around Aguilar Del Alfambra The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra del Pobo and Camino de los Pilones – circular from Aguilar de Alfambra, a 34.5 miles (55.5 km) trail that takes 5 hours 1 minute to complete. This difficult route explores the Sierra del Pobo mountain range. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alcalá de la Selva – Alcalá de la Selva Castle loop from Allepuz, a difficult 29.0 miles (46.7 km) path. This route includes historical landmarks and varied terrain around Alcalá de la Selva. Local touring cyclists also love the Puerto de Sollavientos – Villarroya de los Pinares loop from Aliaga, a 36.3 miles (58.5 km) trail leading through mountainous landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 42 minutes. Touring cycling around Aguilar Del Alfambra is defined by river gorges, unique pollard willows, and varied mountain ranges. The network offers options for different ability levels, though all listed routes are considered difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Aguilar Del Alfambra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Aguilar Del Alfambra?
A: Based on komoot data, there are 5 touring cycling routes in the area. All of them are considered difficult, offering a challenging experience for cyclists.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Aguilar Del Alfambra?
A: All touring cycling routes listed for Aguilar Del Alfambra are rated as &lt;strong&gt;difficult&lt;/strong&gt;. They often involve significant elevation changes and varied terrains, including mountain ranges and river gorges, making them suitable for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the region?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955756139/sierra-del-pobo-and-camino-de-los-pilones-circular-from-aguilar-de-alfambra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Pobo and Camino de los Pilones – circular from Aguilar de Alfambra&lt;/a&gt; offers a challenging loop through the Sierra del Pobo. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31518089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva – Alcalá de la Selva Castle loop from Allepuz&lt;/a&gt;, which explores historical landmarks and varied terrain.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see while cycling in Aguilar Del Alfambra?
A: The region is known for its diverse natural beauty. You can expect to encounter the dramatic 'Cluse del Alfambra' or 'El Estrecho', a geological formation where the Alfambra River has carved a gorge. The area is also part of the Chopo Cabecero Cultural Park, featuring unique 'chopos cabeceros' (pollard willows) along the riverbanks. Varied terrains from rugged sierras to lush riverbanks are common.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. The village of Aguilar del Alfambra itself has historical sights like the 17th-century town hall, the Church of San Pedro Apóstol, and the Fuente del Bacio. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31518089" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de la Selva – Alcalá de la Selva Castle loop from Allepuz&lt;/a&gt; specifically includes historical landmarks. You might also pass through areas with remains of ancient castles and hermitages.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Aguilar Del Alfambra?
A: Aguilar del Alfambra offers diverse landscapes that can be enjoyed across seasons. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference for weather, spring and autumn generally provide milder temperatures ideal for cycling. Summers can be warm, and winters may bring colder conditions, especially in the higher elevations of the Sierra del Pobo.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by bike?
A: Absolutely. The region offers impressive views, particularly from the hermitage of Virgen de la Peña, which provides panoramic vistas of the multi-colored fields and poplar rows along the Alfambra River. The dramatic 'El Estrecho' gorge is another natural spectacle. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cedrillas – Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint loop from Cedrillas&lt;/a&gt; are designed to lead to scenic spots.

Q: What kind of terrain should I expect on these touring cycling routes?
A: The terrain is quite varied, ranging from the ruggedness of the Sierra del Pobo and the Alfambra cliffs to the greener riverbanks. You'll encounter forest and livestock landscapes, as well as cereal fields and clayey terrains. The routes are generally challenging, reflecting the diverse topography of the Teruel province.

Q: Are there any specific attractions or points of interest I should look out for?
A: Beyond the routes themselves, you can explore several points of interest. Consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357676" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villarroya de los Pinares&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5151592" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Field&lt;/a&gt;, or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2979382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorge of the Río de la Val&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5357666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pilones Trail&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable historical site.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Aguilar Del Alfambra?
A: Cyclists using komoot often appreciate the challenging nature of the routes and the stunning natural scenery. The unique geological formations, like the Cluse del Alfambra, and the picturesque 'chopos cabeceros' along the river are frequently highlighted as memorable aspects of the region's touring cycling experience.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through the Chopo Cabecero Cultural Park?
A: Aguilar del Alfambra is situated within the Chopo Cabecero Cultural Park, so many routes will offer views of this unique landscape. The Alfambra River, with its distinctive pollard willows, is a central feature of the area, and cycling along its banks provides a great way to experience the park's natural beauty.

Q: What is the longest touring cycling route available?
A: The longest touring cycling route listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39558855" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cedrillas – Peñarroya Summit Viewpoint loop from Cedrillas&lt;/a&gt;, which spans approximately 92.9 kilometers (57.7 miles) and involves significant elevation gain, making it a substantial challenge.
tips: It is worth the climb for the views with the different landscape contrasts.

A remote and unique area that every bicycle lover should visit if they never have the chance. These are the Parameas de las Tierras Altas, one of the highlights of the bikepacking route through Alto Alfambra and Sierra del Pobo.
It has a bar-restaurant. There is no accommodation. After a kilometer, following the route, the views of the town are beautiful.
You can see the pylons that marked the path of the communication route for the wool trade, in the Middle Ages.

faqs.question: Where does the Sierra del Pobo and Camino de los Pilones circular route start?
faqs.answer: This circular touring cycling route begins and ends in Aguilar de Alfambra. While specific parking details aren't provided, Aguilar de Alfambra serves as the primary access point for the trail.

faqs.question: Is this touring cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is classified as difficult for touring cycling. It features significant elevation changes, diverse surfaces including rocky areas and rougher sections, and may require good fitness. It is not consistently easy and some parts might even necessitate pushing your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: You will encounter a diverse landscape, including rocky areas, extensive pine forests, and high plateaus known as "parameras". The route also follows river valleys, with sections alongside the Alfambra river featuring distinctive "chopos cabeceros" (pollard poplars). While some paths are wide gravel tracks, the ancient Pilones Trail can be rugged and challenging in places.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The trail's remote nature and varied terrain suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most pleasant cycling conditions, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential snow or harsh weather in winter, especially given the high plateaus and historical use of the pilones for guidance in challenging weather.

faqs.question: What natural features and landmarks will I see along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers impressive natural and historical sights. You'll pass through spectacular areas like the Salador Valley and the Baronía de Escriche, which includes abandoned farmhouses. Key highlights include the Viewpoint next to the Ermita de la Peña, the unique landscape of Las Parameas de las Tierras Altas, and the historic Pilones Trail with its ancient stone monoliths.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog restrictions for this route is not available. However, as a general rule for outdoor activities in natural areas, dogs are often permitted but should be kept under control, preferably on a leash, to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other users.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that a permit or entrance fee is required to access this touring cycling route. It appears to be open to the public without such restrictions.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of other trails. You will find it intersects with parts of the Alto Guadalope Enduro trail and several segments of the Camino de los Pilones, which is a significant historical path in itself.

faqs.question: What makes the Camino de los Pilones historically significant?
faqs.answer: The Camino de los Pilones is an ancient bridle path with centuries of history. It was a vital wool trade route and was even used by King James I in the Middle Ages. Its distinctive feature is the series of stone monoliths, or "pilones," some over two meters tall, which historically guided travelers through challenging weather conditions like snow and fog.

faqs.question: What amenities are available in Aguilar de Alfambra?
faqs.answer: Aguilar de Alfambra is a charming village that serves as the starting and end point for this route. While specific details on cafes, pubs, or accommodation are not provided, as a local settlement (El Pobo is another settlement along the way), it would be the most likely place to find basic services before or after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a difficult touring cycling route like this?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and remote nature, you should bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bicycle, appropriate cycling gear for varied terrain and weather conditions, and navigation tools. Good physical fitness is also essential for tackling the significant climbs and diverse surfaces.
